_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Evaluate software design plan timelines and cost estimates
reference_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate build-versus-buy decisions for ML platform components with clear criteria based on organizational maturity, team capability, and long-term operational cost.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Ability AI-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-013 (AI, cosine: 0.475), OP-T-001 (OP, cosine: 0.474).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.397). Shared vocabulary: [cost, evaluate]. NICE-only terms [design, estimates, plan, software, timelines] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, based, build, buy, capability, clear] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.867 (P=0.882, R=0.852). P exceeds R by 0.030 — the NICE element's 8 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Evaluate software design plan timelines and cost estimates" and "Ability to evaluate build-versus-buy decisions for ML platform components with c" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.491 > STS 0.397 (gap +0.094). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [cost, evaluate] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.0%. Shared tokens: [cost, evaluate]. NICE-only: [design, estimates, plan, software, timelines]. WIDAI-only: [ability, based, build, buy, capability, clear].
